ab: aki ta’ mkita’ babaw kinryax suxan ma’, smwayal su’?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: aw, pkita’ ta’ inu’ pi?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: nanu’ mkta’ ta’ sa pqwasan biru’ ma? psyasing ta’.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: nanu’ hata’ squ pslyan na ’tayal, plukus saku’ lukus na ’Tayal.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: m’phpah qu lapaw na qalang myan ga blaq balay ktan, mha ni m’phpah lga ’wah mcisal qalang myan la!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: siki tehuk qu tngawan abaw, iyat hazi’ pnaga kya’ la!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: nanu’ ’wah squ pslkotas mayn la!
ch: W: 那你感恩祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Thanksgiving Festival!

ab: aw ga! pnaga’ saku’ balay ay!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: swa’ su’ mbzinah musa’ maki’ qalang rgyax lozi?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: musa’ saku’ pcbaq biru’ lelaqi’ na pqwasan biru’ qalang myan.
ch: M: 我要回部落教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: swa’ su’ ini’ pcbaq biru’ te hugal pi?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: baha hmswa’ ga sm’inu’ saku’ qalang maku’, ru aki’ maku’ ’benahun miq qalang maku’ qu pinqbaqan maku’.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: baq su’ balay tminun, ima’ pincbaq isu’?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: yaki’ maku’, hya’ ga spzyang balay baq tminun nqu qalang myan.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: swa’ su’ lmnglung mha pqbaqay ta’ qu tminun?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: tminun hiya’ ga gaga’ nqu qnxan ta’ nanak, aki’ saku’ nyux ’mubuy squ gaga’ na qnxan ta’.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: talagay kinblaq nbun qu qwaw trakis qani!
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: kinbalay maku’ nanak ay!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: baq su’ balay wah! bsyaq la? sni’ su qwaw qani?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: ini’ kbsyaq, ki’a hazi smka’ byacing lga blaq la. psklwan ta’ rhkil ini’ ga trakis ru shzi’ sa bluku’ ru mbasaw lga s’imaw na tamul ru, mukan na abaw bgazyaw, mktzyu’ lga tkron sa salaw la.
ch: W:不用，大約兩週就好，先把糯米或小米蒸熟倒入圓型盛器晾乾，並放入酵母菌用姑婆芋蓋住，三天後就裝入甕裡密封。
en: No, It takes about two weeks. to steam the glutinous rice or millet and pour it into a round container to dry, then put in the yeast and cover it with a giant taro, then put it into an urn and seal it after three days.
